examples.text: إِذَا غَامَرْتَ فِي شَرَفٍ مَرُومٍ / فَلَا تَقْنَعْ بِمَا دُونَ النُّجُومِ
examples.english: If you adventured [the world] with noble dignity / Do not be contented with what is less prestigious than the stars

senses.gloss: to allow oneself to be convinced, to be convinced
to be humble, to be content with little
to solicit, to pray humbly, to beg
to put the orifice of a bag to one's mouth in order to drink
to veil, to put on the veil قِنَاع (qināʕ)
verbal noun of قَنِعَ (qaniʕa, “to be contented, to be convinced”) (form I)
verbal noun of قَنَعَ (qanaʕa, “to put the orifice of a bag to one's mouth to drink”) (form I)
